zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de Neville於1405年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾洛勒斯頓時，他的父親Robert de Neville41歲，母親Alice de Longford41歲。他於大約1420年與Elizabeth Babington在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1482年5月22日在他的家鄉享年77歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Neville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevills
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Nevil
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Newill
meanings: English and Irish (or Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevill
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevels
meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de Neville was born in 1405, in Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Robert de Neville, was 41 and his mother, Alice de Longford, was 41. He married Elizabeth Babington about 1420, in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 8 daughters. He died on 22 May 1482, in his hometown, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas de Neville est né en 1405, à Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Neville, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Alice de Longford, avait 41 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Babington environ 1420, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 8 filles. Il est décédé le 22 mai 1482, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 77 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de Nevilleが1405年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアロレストンで生まれた時，父Robert de Nevilleは41歳，母Alice de Longfordは41歳であった。約1420年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアでElizabeth Babingtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1482年5月22日に故郷で77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de Neville(이)가 1405년 영국 노틴그함실르 롤레스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Neville(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Longford(은)는 41세였다. 그는 대략 1420년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르에서 Elizabeth Babington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1482년 5월 22일 그의 고향에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.
